prompt:
i want to check if this was done 

The user is unavailable. I'll commit to the recommended options and produce the plan autonomously. Decisions:

Blackbox PDF: I'll use the public Blackbox.ai homepage as the visual/structural reference during the marketing rebuild.
Screenshot input: Xupra Pro AI only (vision tokens billed to you only for Pro users).
Clarifying questions: One round, 2–4 questions, then plan.
Enterprise mode: Auto-detect — if Pro API unreachable, silently fall back to External AI Prompt with a banner.
Shipping: Slice-by-slice — each ticket = commit + version bump + push.
Plan: Zero-Friction Build Sessions + Marketing Rebuild
End-to-end build that delivers your vision: paste prompt/screenshot → AI asks 2–4 clarifying questions → planner generates a runbook → kanban with visible per-step status → drag to reorder → pick agent → click Handoff → tick steps off → next phase auto-activates. Plus Blackbox-style marketing rebuild and enterprise/air-gapped fallback. Ships in slices: each ticket = commit + extension version bump + push.

Phases

A — Provider transparency (T1)
B — Step model migration (T2)
C — Kanban execution UX (T3, T4, T5)
D — Clarifying-question loop (T6)
E — Multimodal paste (T7)
F — Enterprise / air-gapped fallback (T8)
G — Marketing rebuild (T9, parallel)
Tickets

T1 — Planning AI status banner (Control Room + sidebar). Provider resolver returns {provider, reason}. → 0.2.6
T2 — XuStep type with per-step status; normalizeSteps() keeps legacy string[] runbooks working. → 0.2.7
T3 — Render steps as a checklist inside each phase card; checkbox toggles pending → active → complete. → 0.2.8
T4 — Per-phase Handoff button with agent-specific routing (Claude Code opens file, Cursor copies for Composer, Copilot opens chat, Codex copies, External opens side-by-side). → 0.2.9
T5 — Auto-advance: phase complete → next phase active; "Continue" always points to active phase. → 0.3.0
T6 — Clarifying-question chat loop. New POST /api/v1/drylake/runbooks/clarify. Provider gets clarify() method. → 0.3.1
T7 — Screenshot paste in prompt textarea. Pro-only vision; LM/clipboard ignore images with notice. → 0.3.2
T8 — Enterprise mode: drylake.forceExternalAiPrompt setting + health-probe fallback. → 0.3.3
T9 — Marketing homepage rebuild in Blackbox.ai's visual idiom (dark hero, agent logos, three-step explainer, pricing). Parallel.
Relevant files — full list saved in memories/session/plan.md. Key paths:

providerResolver.ts, DryLakeAiProvider.ts, provider implementations
types.ts, parseXu.ts, renderXu.ts
controlRoomProvider.ts, commands/runbooks.ts
New: app/api/v1/drylake/runbooks/clarify/route.ts
page.tsx, app-home.tsx

npm run typecheck && npm test && npm run build in extension
npm run package:vsix && npm run install:vsix; confirm new version; clean VSIX
Commit + push to origin/development (auto-deploys server to staging)

Decisions locked autonomously

Further considerations

Step ID format ${phase.id}.${index+1} (e.g. 1.1) for legacy migration — flag if you want different (P1-S1 etc.).
T8 health probe adds ~3s startup latency; I'll cache the result 60s per session to keep startup snappy.
Marketing demo GIF/video — record in final pass after T1–T5 are live so the demo matches the new UX. Static screenshots until then.
